XOnlineStatReadGetResult

Retrieves the statistics data requested by calling XOnlineStatRead.

HRESULT XOnlineStatReadGetResult(
    XONLINETASK_HANDLE hTask,
    DWORD dwNumStatSpecs,
    PXONLINE_STAT_SPEC pStatSpecs,
    DWORD dwExtraBufferSize,
    BYTE *pExtraBuffer
);

Parameters

hTask
[in] Handle to the task returned by XOnlineStatRead.
dwNumStatSpecs
[in] Number of XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures pointed to by the pStatSpecs parameter.
pStatSpecs
[out] Pointer to an array of XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures. Each structure receives the statistics for a single player. The player is specified by the xuidUser member of the structure. The pStats member points to an array of XONLINE_STAT structures that store the statistics.
dwExtraBufferSize
[in] Size, in bytes, of the buffer pointed to by the pExtraBuffer parameter.
pExtraBuffer
[in, out] Pointer to a buffer that receives variable-length data associated with the retrieved statistics. See the Remarks section for more information.

Return Values

If XOnlineStatReadGetResult succeeds, the return value is S_OK.

If XOnlineStatReadGetResult fails, the return value is one of the following error codes.

Value Description
E_FAIL Unspecified failure. If you receive this error, try the following:
  • Check your arguments to this function.
  • Contact Support.
  • If this happens in a game, try again later.
Other See Online Errors for information about other possible errors.

Remarks

The number, order, and contents of the XONLINE_STAT_SPEC and XONLINE_STAT structures passed to XOnlineStatReadGetResult must be the same as that passed to XOnlineStatRead. (The pStats member of each XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structure points to an array of XONLINE_STAT structures.)

The XOnlineStatReadGetResult function always returns title-defined statistics in the order specified by the title in the call to XOnlineStatRead. The reserved attributes are always returned last. The reserved attributes are Rank, Rating, Leaderboard Size, and Nickname.

Note    Leaderboards are also referred to as scoreboards.

If the statistic specified by the wID member is not found, the statistics service sets the type member to XONLINE_STAT_NONE.

The buffer pointed to by the pExtraBuffer parameter receives variable-length data associated with the retrieved statistics. This buffer stores player nicknames and statistics attachment paths. Use the following formula to calculate how much space to allocate for this buffer:

dwExtraBufferSize

= <number_of_requested_nicknames> * XONLINE_STAT_MAX_NICKNAME_LENGTH * sizeof(WCHAR)

+ <number_of_requested_attachment_paths> * XONLINESTORAGE_MAX_PATH * sizeof(WCHAR)

The title does not need to parse the data in the buffer; the lpString member of XONLINE_STAT points directly to the location of the string in the buffer.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineStatRead
